title: Wage rate requirements for visas :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Wage rate requirements for visas
For some work visas you need to earn more than a certain amount to get the visa. Check if you meet the wage threshold to qualify for the visa you want to apply for.

## What is a wage threshold
Some visas require you to be paid a certain amount — we call this the wage threshold for a visa. We check the pay rate that you have been offered for a job, to see if it meets the wage threshold for the visa you are applying for.

### If the wage threshold for your visa increases
If you already have a visa and the wage threshold for your visa increases, you do not need to meet the new wage threshold rate. However if you apply for another visa, you will need to meet the new wage threshold.

## Wage thresholds we use
Some immigration wage and income thresholds are based on the . The median wage rate we use changes each year. When the median wage increases, wage thresholds which are based on the median wage will also increase.
The wage rate or threshold that applies to you will depend on what visa you have and when you got it.
Labour market statistics (income): June 2025 quarter — Stats NZ
All wage rates shown on here are hourly rates and are in New Zealand dollars.
Use the median wage calculator to work out your hourly wage rate: Median wage comparison calculator
The New Zealand is a range of pay that a New Zealander or resident would be paid within to do the job, or equivalent work. For example, the market rate pay range for a job could be between NZD $27 and NZD $30 an hour.

### Other wage thresholds
Some wage thresholds are based on or indexed to:
- the New Zealand minimum wage
- other occupation-specific wage rates (for example, wage rates from pay equity settlements).

## Pay thresholds for the different visa types
- AEWV jobs must pay at least the for the job. They must also meet any other legal pay requirements, such as paying the New Zealand minimum wage. We approve a pay range at the job check stage, which must be at or above the pay threshold. The actual pay rate on the visa is determined at the visa application stage. How we calculate pay rates for the Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) If you have an AEWV, check if you are able to support a visa for your partner or dependent child. The type of visa of visas you can support will depend on your job and how much you earn. Bringing family if you have an AEWV
AEWV jobs must pay at least the for the job. They must also meet any other legal pay requirements, such as paying the New Zealand minimum wage.
We approve a pay range at the job check stage, which must be at or above the pay threshold. The actual pay rate on the visa is determined at the visa application stage.
How we calculate pay rates for the Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV)
If you have an AEWV, check if you are able to support a visa for your partner or dependent child. The type of visa of visas you can support will depend on your job and how much you earn.
Bringing family if you have an AEWV
- To qualify for this visa, you need to: currently be paid at least NZD$28.25 an hour (or the equivalent salary) worked for 24 months in this sector, and been paid at least the care workforce sector wage rate during that time Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa Table 1: Care workforce sector agreement wage rates Date range From 1 July 2022 (in NZD) From 1 July 2022 $28.25 29 September 2021 to 30 June 2022 $27.00
To qualify for this visa, you need to:
- currently be paid at least NZD$28.25 an hour (or the equivalent salary)
- worked for 24 months in this sector, and
- been paid at least the care workforce sector wage rate during that time
Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa
From 1 July 2022 (in NZD)
29 September 2021 to 30 June 2022
- To qualify for this visa you need to meet the following criteria. You must be paid at least the median wage (or the bus drivers wage rate, if applicable) when you apply. You must have worked for 24 months in this sector. For 24 out of the 30 months before you apply, you must have been paid at least the median wage (or the bus drivers wage rate, if applicable). You can use the median wage rate in place at the start of your 24 months — this may be different to the current median wage. Use Table 3 to check the previous wage rates and when they were used. Transport Work to Residence Visa Check if you can include past work experience The following jobs were removed from the list of approved of transport sector roles on 7 April 2024: Bus Driver (ANZSCO code 731211) Truck Driver (ANZSCO code 733111) Aircraft Refueller (ANZSCO code 733112) Furniture Removalist (ANZSCO code 733113) Tanker Driver (ANZSCO code 733114) Tow Truck Driver (ANZSCO code 733115) You may still be able to count work experience gained from a removed job towards your residence visa, if on or before 6 April 2024 you: started work in 1 of these jobs received an AEWV to work in one of these jobs, or applied for an AEWV to work in one of these jobs, and were successful in your application. Table 2: Transport Work to Residence wage rates Jobs From 9 March 2026 (in NZD) Ship’s Master (ANZSCO code 231213) $35.00 Deck hand (ANZSCO code 899211 ) $35.00 Table 3: Previous Transport Work to Residence median wage rates Date range Wage rate (in NZD) Bus driver rate (in NZD) 18 August 2025 to 8 March 2026 $33.56 $28 28 February 2024 to 17 August 2025 $31.61 $28 27 February 2023 to 27 February 2024 $29.66 $28 4 July 2022 to 26 February 2023 $27.76 $28 29 September 2021 to 3 July 2022 $27.00 $28
To qualify for this visa you need to meet the following criteria.
- You must be paid at least the median wage (or the bus drivers wage rate, if applicable) when you apply.
- You must have worked for 24 months in this sector.
- For 24 out of the 30 months before you apply, you must have been paid at least the median wage (or the bus drivers wage rate, if applicable). You can use the median wage rate in place at the start of your 24 months — this may be different to the current median wage. Use Table 3 to check the previous wage rates and when they were used.
Transport Work to Residence Visa

#### Check if you can include past work experience
The following jobs were removed from the list of approved of transport sector roles on 7 April 2024:
- Bus Driver (ANZSCO code 731211)
- Truck Driver (ANZSCO code 733111)
- Aircraft Refueller (ANZSCO code 733112)
- Furniture Removalist (ANZSCO code 733113)
- Tanker Driver (ANZSCO code 733114)
- Tow Truck Driver (ANZSCO code 733115)
You may still be able to count work experience gained from a removed job towards your residence visa, if on or before 6 April 2024 you:
- started work in 1 of these jobs
- received an AEWV to work in one of these jobs, or
- applied for an AEWV to work in one of these jobs, and were successful in your application.

Pay rates for the Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa
- There is a minimum earning threshold if you want to support your parents on a Parent Resident Visa. As a sponsor, you must earn more than the threshold for 2 out of the 3 years before their expression of interest is selected. The threshold is: tied to the median wage depends on how any sponsors and parents are in the application. The Parent Resident Visa is updated in February each year. Parent Resident Visa sponsor income requirements
There is a minimum earning threshold if you want to support your parents on a Parent Resident Visa. As a sponsor, you must earn more than the threshold for 2 out of the 3 years before their expression of interest is selected.
- tied to the median wage
- depends on how any sponsors and parents are in the application.
The Parent Resident Visa is updated in February each year.
Parent Resident Visa sponsor income requirements
- If you have an AEWV you may be able to support a Partner of a Worker Work Visa for your partner, depending on how much you earn and your job. Bringing family if you have an AEWV There is no income requirement if you have a different work visa and you can support a visa for your partner. Partner of a Worker Work Visa
